---
subtitle: "Tools 2"
title: "Day 1 - Block 5"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D1 B5"
    bibliography: ../../../resources/workshop-citations.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

## Multi-agent needs orchestration {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Parallelism without contracts creates chaos.
:::

## Objective

- Use advanced tool patterns
- Run parallel agents safely
- Customize AGENTS.md constraints

## Parallel strategy

- Split tasks by boundary
- Merge with explicit contracts
- Keep one shared verifier

## Tree-of-thought to agent branches

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  I[Input task] --> A[Branch A]
  I --> B[Branch B]
  I --> C[Branch C]
  B --> D[Best branch selected]
  D --> E[Verifier check]
```

- Branch only with selection rules
- Keep logs for reproducibility

## Self-feedback loop

- Generate a first pass
- Ask model for specific critique
- Revise once, then stop on criteria

## Backlog scheduling

- Queue small, testable issues
- Prioritize risk and dependency
- Use idle windows for low-risk

## AGENTS.md customization (example)

```yaml
code_style:
  prefer: explicit assumptions
  require: reproducible scripts
  avoid: hidden state
testing:
  require: at least one check per change
handoff:
  require: residual risk note
```

## Hands-on

- Run two agents in parallel on independent subtasks
- Merge outputs through one verifier
- Submit coordination note + verification trace

## Registry references {.smaller}

- Agentic engineering patterns: R004
- Architecture comparisons: R003
- Coding-assistance evidence: R012

## Common failure modes

- Agents overwrite each other
- Missing merge contract
- No shared acceptance tests

## Debrief

- Which split paid off?
- Which split increased risk?
- What to simplify next run?
